title: From Self To Others
study: A Study In The Life Of Moses
scriptureForWeek: EXODUS 19:1 - 20:21 Exactly two months after the Israelites left Egypt, they arrived in the wilderness of Sinai. After breaking camp at Rephidim, they came to the wilderness of Sinai and set up camp there at the base of Mount Sinai. Then Moses climbed the mountain to appear before God. The LORD called to him from the mountain and said, “Give these instructions to the family of Jacob; announce it to the descendants of Israel: ‘You have seen what I did to the Egyptians. You know how I carried you on eagles’ wings and brought you to myself. Now if you will obey me and keep my covenant, you will be my own special treasure from among all the peoples on earth; for all the earth belongs to me. And you will be my kingdom of priests, my holy nation.’ This is the message you must give to the people of Israel.” So Moses returned from the mountain and called together the elders of the people and told them everything the LORD had commanded him. And all the people responded together, “We will do everything the LORD has commanded.” So Moses brought the people’s answer back to the LORD. Then the LORD said to Moses, “I will come to you in a thick cloud, Moses, so the people themselves can hear me when I speak with you. Then they will always trust you.” Moses told the LORD what the people had said. Then the LORD told Moses, “Go down and prepare the people for my arrival. Consecrate them today and tomorrow, and have them wash their clothing. Be sure they are ready on the third day, for on that day the LORD will come down on Mount Sinai as all the people watch. Mark off a boundary all around the mountain. Warn the people, ‘Be careful! Do not go up on the mountain or even touch its boundaries. Anyone who touches the mountain will certainly be put to death. No hand may touch the person or animal that crosses the boundary; instead, stone them or shoot them with arrows. They must be put to death.’ However, when the ram’s horn sounds a long blast, then the people may go up on the mountain.”
weekTheme: Boys Ignore, Men Invite How does a man relate to God? How does he help others have a relationship with God? These are some of the most important questions you will ever ask. At the end of the day, the reason you were put here on earth is to know God and to make him known. But lots of people have a distorted view of what it means to know and enjoy God. In a large study done with over 3000 teenagers, they defined a relationship with God by these five things. 1- A God exists who created and ordered the world and watches over human life on earth. 2- God wants people to be good, nice, and fair to each other, as taught in the Bible and by most world religions. 3- The central goal of life is to be happy and to feel good about oneself. 4- God does not need to be particularly involved in one's life except when God is needed to resolve a problem. 5- Good people go to heaven when they die. They called these 5 views Moralistic Therapeutic Deism. Now those are some big, funky words! Sadly these 5 views DON’T reflect what the bible teaches about what a relationship with God is actually like. This is a sad and cheap distortion of what a true relationship with God is. This week, Moses is going to help the Israelites enter into a proper relationship with the one true God. He will teach us how God defines a relationship and what is important to him. There will be smoke, there will be fire, there will be truth. Let’s go.

days.title: A Proper Relationship
days.scripture: DEUTERONOMY 7:6-9 For you are a holy people, who belong to the LORD your God. Of all the people on earth, the LORD your God has chosen you to be his own special treasure. “The LORD did not set his heart on you and choose you because you were more numerous than other nations, for you were the smallest of all nations! Rather, it was simply that the LORD loves you, and he was keeping the oath he had sworn to your ancestors. That is why the LORD rescued you with such a strong hand from your slavery and from the oppressive hand of Pharaoh, king of Egypt. Understand, therefore, that the LORD your God is indeed God. He is the faithful God who keeps his covenant for a thousand generations and lavishes his unfailing love on those who love him and obey his commands.
days.reflection: Relationships with other people can be weird. It’s hard to understand how they work or how to start one. This can be particularly true for guys your age. It can be hard to meet new people, figure out if you are actually friends, and worry about rejection. Guys your age can be pretty mean and ruthless too, so it makes it extra challenging. The good news is, God isn’t like that. Although it can be hard to relate to someone who is invisible, when you understand who God is, you will see he wants nothing more than for you to know his love and care. But relating to God isn’t the same as relating to an earthly friend. It’s a different kind of relationship, called a covenant. There aren’t many of these left in our world today. But a covenant is an exclusive and sacred commitment. It’s kind of like a marriage. But not everyone wants to relate to God with that kind of commitment. Some guys settle for less than God offers. They choose to relate to God at different levels. The first level of commitment is a consumer. A consumer is only in the relationship for what the person gives them. As long as God does what they want, they are in. The second he doesn’t, they are out. The second level of commitment is casual. They like the person, but don’t take things too seriously. They feel free to do what they want when they want, but pop into things with God when they feel like it. The third level is commitment. This is when you take your relationship with God seriously. Your faith means a lot to you. God is one of your real priorities. As good as this is, there is a level of depth and friendship deeper than this. It’s the covenantal level. The fourth level is Covenant. In a covenant relationship with God, he shows you his love, kindness, and help. He adopts you into his family and shares his heart with you. He reveals his will, plans, and purposes for your life. In response, you commit to him. A covenant relationship is an exclusive, loyal, wholehearted commitment to God. You are committed no matter what it costs you or what others think. God is not one of your priorities; He is the priority. This is the kind of relationship God wants with you. God is the most committed, loyal, and loving person you will ever meet. He created you to know him, he gave you his word so you could understand him, he sent his son into the world so you could be reconciled to him, and he sent his Spirit into your heart so you could walk with him. This week, we are going to see how Moses leads the children of Israel to meet with God. God will enter a covenant with them to be their God, and they will be his people. This is kind of like a marriage. Men who live for others will seek to lead people to know God too. Tomorrow, Moses will show you exactly what that looks like.
questions: Who is one of your closest friends? What makes this person fun to hang out with?
Of the 4 levels of relationship with God, which one do you think best describes your relationship with him? If none of those words work, how else would you describe it?

Ask the person you are doing this study with to share how they learned to grow in commitment to God. What were a few of the moments that helped them start taking their relationship with God seriously?

thoughts.verse: Do not be afraid or discouraged, for the LORD will personally go ahead of you. He will be with you; he will neither fail you nor abandon you.”
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to show you his covenant love. Thank him that he wants to know you and walk with you.

days.title: Willing To Obey
days.scripture: EXODUS 19:1-6 Exactly two months after the Israelites left Egypt, they arrived in the wilderness of Sinai. After breaking camp at Rephidim, they came to the wilderness of Sinai and set up camp there at the base of Mount Sinai. Then Moses climbed the mountain to appear before God. The LORD called to him from the mountain and said, “Give these instructions to the family of Jacob; announce it to the descendants of Israel: ‘You have seen what I did to the Egyptians. You know how I carried you on eagles’ wings and brought you to myself. Now if you will obey me and keep my covenant, you will be my own special treasure from among all the peoples on earth; for all the earth belongs to me. And you will be my kingdom of priests, my holy nation.’ This is the message you must give to the people of Israel.”
days.reflection: When you are in a relationship with someone, you have to learn to think about them. You have to consider their opinions, their desires, and their needs. That’s the heart of what it means to really love someone. The same is true in our relationship with God. If we really love him, we will care about what he wants. This is called obedience. But it’s not a legalistic obedience where we have to love God or he will hate us. It’s a loving obedience that responds to how much he loves us. 1 John 4:19 says, We love him, because he first loved us. We don’t obey God so he will love us, we obey him because he does love us. We are not trying to get his favor; we obey him because in Jesus we already have his favor. A shallow man will obey God as long as God does what he wants. A religious man will obey God to try and get God to do what he wants. A Christian will obey God because he cares about what God wants. We don’t obey God because we have to. We obey God because we want to. When God enters into a covenant with the Israelites, he reminds them why they should respond in loving obedience. 1- Obey God because he set you free ‘You have seen what I did to the Egyptians. God delivered the Israelites from terrible oppression and slavery in Egypt. God delivers us from slavery to sin, Satan, death, and hell. Out of gratitude for that, we obey. 2- Obey God because he is kind to you You know how I carried you on eagles’ wings and brought you to myself. God provided for the Israelites and brought them to himself. God sent Jesus to bring us to the Father. He came to show us God's love and prepare a place for us to enjoy him forever. 3- Obey God because you are special to him Now if you will obey me and keep my covenant, you will be my own special treasure from among all the peoples on earth; for all the earth belongs to me. Everything belongs to God; it’s part of what makes him God. But even he has things he likes the most, things he treasures. The crazy thing is, that’s us, his people. We obey him out of gratitude and wonder that he would love and value us so much in a world that couldn’t really care less about us. 4- Obey God because you have a special calling And you will be my kingdom of priests, my holy nation.’ The Israelites understood this, and they replied to God, “We will do everything the LORD has commanded.” So Moses brought the people’s answer back to the LORD. God wanted the Israelites to show the rest of the world how good and kind he was. They were given a special task of representing him to the world. You do too. You are called to help people see the grace and mercy of Jesus and how they can enjoy God's love. Obedience can freak people out. In our selfish world, a lot of guys want to do whatever they want, whenever they want. But they are going to miss out. Miss out on grace, miss out on favor, miss out on calling. In light of all God has done for you, obedience is the only proper response. Living that out and helping others find freedom in a covenant with God is what real men do.
questions: Do you think you have to obey someone to love them? Why or why not?

Lots of guys think about what they have to give up to be in a relationship with God, but they forget about everything they get when they have a relationship with God. How can remembering what God has given us help us when we feel like we are missing out?

Do you think you can explain the difference between obeying God so he will love us, versus obeying God because he loves us? Give it a try. Then ask the person you are doing this study with to share it too!

thoughts.verse: Those who accept my commandments and obey them are the ones who love me. And because they love me, my Father will love them. And I will love them and reveal myself to each of them.”
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to thank God for his incredible love. Ask him to give you a willing heart to obey all that he wants you to. If you sense him prompting you to obey something, or maybe you have disobeyed, take a moment to ask for his forgiveness and help.

days.title: Willing To Listen
days.scripture: EXODUS 19:9 Then the LORD said to Moses, “I will come to you in a thick cloud, Moses, so the people themselves can hear me when I speak with you. Then they will always trust you.”
days.reflection: What do you think the voice of God sounds like? Do you think it's high-pitched or low-pitched? Does God have an accent? Walking with God is not just about believing a book written thousands of years ago and trying to be good. It’s hearing his voice and understanding his heart. Jesus said, My sheep hear my voice and they follow me. The Bible places a massive emphasis on listening to God and hearing his voice. That means you have to learn to listen. That means you have to help others listen to the right voices too. The children of Israel have been listening to all sorts of voices over the years. They have been listening to the voice of Pharaoh telling them they were worthless slaves. They have been listening to the voice of their taskmasters telling them to work harder. They have been listening to the voices of the Egyptian magicians telling them to serve and fear the wrong gods. Now, God wants them to learn to hear his voice. He wants his voice to be the loudest voice in their heads. So Moses gathers the people together so that can happen. Then the LORD said to Moses, “I will come to you in a thick cloud, Moses, so the people themselves can hear me when I speak with you. Then they will always trust you.” God speaks to us to tell us the truth and free us from lies. He speaks to reveal his heart. He speaks to warn us about the things that will hurt us. He speaks to clear up any confusion. He does this because he loves us and wants what’s best for us. So, we should listen. We should strain our ears to hear his voice above all the noise in our world today. Moses wanted the children of Israel to listen to God. You should want your friends to listen to God too. If you are going to live for others, you will care about who they listen to. You will care about what they believe. You will care about which voices have the most influence in their lives. This is really important because you live in a world filled with lies. Paul warned Timothy, For a time is coming when people will no longer listen to sound and wholesome teaching. They will follow their own desires and will look for teachers who will tell them whatever their itching ears want to hear. They will reject the truth and chase after myths. (2 Timothy 4:3-4) One of a parent’s greatest frustrations is when their kids don’t listen. (How many times have you heard “you’re not listening to me”) One of God’s greatest frustrations is when his people stop listening to him. I bet you get frustrated when people don’t listen to you. So listen up. Help others hear. Men always seek to hear the voice of truth.
questions: What voices from the culture do you think guys your age listen to the most? Who are some of the most influential people for people your age? What message are they bringing?

God can speak in all sorts of ways to us. He can speak through the Bible, through other people, through our circumstances, to our hearts and minds - all sorts of ways. Have you ever sensed God speaking to you or prompting you in any way?
Ask the person you are doing this study with to share a time they sensed God speaking to them. How did they know it was God? What did it sound like? How did they respond?

thoughts.verse: My child, listen to what I say, and treasure my commands. Tune your ears to wisdom, and concentrate on understanding. Cry out for insight, and ask for understanding. Search for them as you would for silver; seek them like hidden treasures. Then you will understand what it means to fear the LORD, and you will gain knowledge of God.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to speak to you today. Ask him to turn up the volume of his voice and turn down the noise of the world.

days.title: Wanting His Will
days.scripture: EXODUS 20:1-21 Then God gave the people all these instructions: “I am the LORD your God, who rescued you from the land of Egypt, the place of your slavery. “You must not have any other god but me. “You must not make for yourself an idol of any kind or an image of anything in the heavens or on the earth or in the sea. You must not bow down to them or worship them, for I, the LORD your God, am a jealous God who will not tolerate your affection for any other gods. I lay the sins of the parents upon their children; the entire family is affected—even children in the third and fourth generations of those who reject me. But I lavish unfailing love for a thousand generations on those who love me and obey my commands. “You must not misuse the name of the LORD your God. The LORD will not let you go unpunished if you misuse his name. “Remember to observe the Sabbath day by keeping it holy. You have six days each week for your ordinary work, but the seventh day is a Sabbath day of rest dedicated to the LORD your God. On that day no one in your household may do any work. This includes you, your sons and daughters, your male and female servants, your livestock, and any foreigners living among you. For in six days the LORD made the heavens, the earth, the sea, and everything in them; but on the seventh day he rested. That is why the LORD blessed the Sabbath day and set it apart as holy. “Honor your father and mother. Then you will live a long, full life in the land the LORD your God is giving you. “You must not murder. “You must not commit adultery. “You must not steal. “You must not testify falsely against your neighbor. “You must not covet your neighbor’s house. You must not covet your neighbor’s wife, male or female servant, ox or donkey, or anything else that belongs to your neighbor.” When the people heard the thunder and the loud blast of the ram’s horn, and when they saw the flashes of lightning and the smoke billowing from the mountain, they stood at a distance, trembling with fear. And they said to Moses, “You speak to us, and we will listen. But don’t let God speak directly to us, or we will die!” “Don’t be afraid,” Moses answered them, “for God has come in this way to test you, and so that your fear of him will keep you from sinning!” As the people stood in the distance, Moses approached the dark cloud where God was.
days.reflection: Have you ever been to a wedding? People dress up, cry a bit, and have a formal ceremony. Then there is a time of dancing and celebration, even some decent food. Depending on who is getting married, weddings can be fun. A wedding is the most serious commitment a man and a woman can make to each other. They are standing before God, their family, and their friends, making a covenant of faithfulness to each other. The vows are actually the most important part of the ceremony because that’s where the commitment is. Without the commitment, there is just a bunch of cake, fancy clothes, and music. God is serious about his relationship with Israel. He loves them as a people with deep passion. So much so that he wants to enter into a covenant with them. This is the deepest commitment a person can make, a covenant with God. This commitment is summarized in the 10 commandments. (There is a lot more details in Deuteronomy 28-30, but you can read that on your own.) Though the 10 commandments can sound religious and moral, they are actually relational in nature. There are a couple of things to notice about the 10 commandments God gives his people. First, these commands are a response to God’s grace, not an effort to earn it. Look at the way God frames them. “I am the LORD your God, who rescued you from the land of Egypt, the place of your slavery. The Israelites were rescued by God's power and love, then invited to obey him. God didn’t say to the Israelites, “Here are the 10 commandments. When you live them perfectly, then I will come and rescue you.” If he had to wait for them to be perfect, they would have been enslaved forever. We are saved for obedience, not by obedience. Second, the first 4 commandments are really just about our relationship with God. We are to be loyal to him above all else, not make up dumb ideas about how he looks, not speak about him disrespectfully, and give him a day of the week of worship. They are deeply relational commands. Thirdly, the last six are simply the ways that we love our neighbor. Honor your parents, and don’t murder, steal, lie, or have sex with someone who is not your spouse. Don’t get jealous and want other people’s stuff. These commands are just love put into practice. These commandments are not some random list of moral commands we have to keep or the policemen in the sky will get angry. They are relational commitments made to the God who loves us. If you are going to help lead others, you are going to have to help them understand the kind of relationship God wants to have with them. You are going to have to invite them into a covenant with God. Men make and keep covenantal commitments. Boys break them. God loves you so much that he has invited you into a deep and loyal relationship with himself. It’s definitely worth celebrating. It’s even better than dressing up and eating cake.
questions: How many of the 10 commandments can you say by heart? Which of the 10 commandments do you think is most challenging for you? Why?
How does the idea of the 10 commandments being relational and not just moral change the way you see them?
Ask the person you are doing this study with about their wedding day. How have they worked to keep their vows over the years? Share one or two stories.

thoughts.verse: How can a young person stay pure? By obeying your word. I have tried hard to find you— don’t let me wander from your commands. I have hidden your word in my heart, that I might not sin against you.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you from it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to really commit yourself to God, thank him for his rescuing grace, and ask him for power to obey in love.

days.title: Reverence For God
days.scripture: EXODUS 19:9-25 Then the LORD said to Moses, “I will come to you in a thick cloud, Moses, so the people themselves can hear me when I speak with you. Then they will always trust you.” Moses told the LORD what the people had said. Then the LORD told Moses, “Go down and prepare the people for my arrival. Consecrate them today and tomorrow, and have them wash their clothing. Be sure they are ready on the third day, for on that day the LORD will come down on Mount Sinai as all the people watch. Mark off a boundary all around the mountain. Warn the people, ‘Be careful! Do not go up on the mountain or even touch its boundaries. Anyone who touches the mountain will certainly be put to death. No hand may touch the person or animal that crosses the boundary; instead, stone them or shoot them with arrows. They must be put to death.’ However, when the ram’s horn sounds a long blast, then the people may go up on the mountain.” So Moses went down to the people. He consecrated them for worship, and they washed their clothes. He told them, “Get ready for the third day, and until then abstain from having sexual intercourse.” On the morning of the third day, thunder roared and lightning flashed, and a dense cloud came down on the mountain. There was a long, loud blast from a ram’s horn, and all the people trembled. Moses led them out from the camp to meet with God, and they stood at the foot of the mountain. All of Mount Sinai was covered with smoke because the LORD had descended on it in the form of fire. The smoke billowed into the sky like smoke from a brick kiln, and the whole mountain shook violently. As the blast of the ram’s horn grew louder and louder, Moses spoke, and God thundered his reply. The LORD came down on the top of Mount Sinai and called Moses to the top of the mountain. So Moses climbed the mountain. Then the LORD told Moses, “Go back down and warn the people not to break through the boundaries to see the LORD, or they will die. Even the priests who regularly come near to the LORD must purify themselves so that the LORD does not break out and destroy them.” “But LORD,” Moses protested, “the people cannot come up to Mount Sinai. You already warned us. You told me, ‘Mark off a boundary all around the mountain to set it apart as holy.’” But the LORD said, “Go down and bring Aaron back up with you. In the meantime, do not let the priests or the people break through to approach the LORD, or he will break out and destroy them.” So Moses went down to the people and told them what the LORD had said.
days.reflection: If you got invited to the Whitehouse to meet the President, how would you feel? What would you wear? What language would you use? I bet you wouldn’t be making dumb jokes, wearing a hoodie with ripped jeans, and running around playing games inside. I bet you would have a sense of honor, respect, and reverence. It wouldn’t even matter who the current President was. You would respect the office of the President, and the history of the house. You live in a world where there is no respect or reverence for God. The only time Jesus is mentioned in our world today is as a swear word when something goes wrong. It’s not much better in the larger Western church today either. A relationship with God is often reduced to something mushy and sentimental that doesn’t reflect the wonder and awe of who he is. We sing about God like he is our buddy, our girlfriend, a bro. This is too low a view of the God who created the universe, the God who makes all of existence possible. The Israelites knew that meeting God was a serious matter. Joyful, but serious. So they had to get ready. Moses told the people to prepare themselves, purify themselves, and consecrate themselves. They were meeting someone important. They had to wash their clothes, stop their normal routine, and get ready. And when they were ready, God showed up. God descended in the form of fire and the whole mountain was covered in smoke. The whole place shook, and God thundered from heaven. The people were in awe. This was also true when the Holy Spirit was poured out in Acts 2. Fire came on the heads of each person, the room shook, and the people were in awe. God may be kind, merciful, and compassionate, but he is still God. He is still awesome and powerful and glorious. In the Chronicles of Narnia, Lucy is kind of nervous to meet Aslan, the Lion and true king of Narnia. She is worried about what he is like. “Then he isn’t safe?” said Lucy. “Safe?” said Mr. Beaver. “Don’t you hear what Mrs. Beaver tells you? Who said anything about safe? ’Course he isn’t safe. But he’s good. He’s the King, I tell you.” If you really care about other people, you are going to care about their spiritual life. You will care that they have respect and reverence for God. You will want them to know the good King, the true king, but he isn’t safe. He has to be taken seriously. He must be treated with reverence. He’s loving, but wild. He’s gracious, but powerful. He’s kind, yet holy. You can’t tame him, control him, or manipulate him. He is God. This week, you have learned how Moses led the Israelites into a relationship with God. If you are going to be a man who lives for others and not a boy who lives for himself, you are going to want to lead people to God. You are going to want them to be set free, find truth, experience forgiveness, overcome temptation, and walk with God. You may not be Moses leading the masses, but you can be a man who leads others to Jesus, and that is something for which you can hold your head high. To be a man is to care about connecting others to our covenant God. You’re doing great. Keep going.
questions: Do you think people in our culture respect God today? Why or why not? Can you give an example? Do you find it easy or hard to talk to other guys your age about faith? Why or why not? Ask the person you are doing this study with how they got the confidence to talk about faith with other people. Ask them to share a story where this went well and when it didn’t.

thoughts.verse: Since we are receiving a Kingdom that is unshakable, let us be thankful and please God by worshiping him with holy fear and awe. For our God is a devouring fire.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to increase your sense of awe and reverence for him. Ask him to give you a chance to talk about him with someone in your world today.

extras: WEEK 8 MANSCHOOL SNAZZY MANSCHOOL REMINDER The recipe for fun is pretty simple when you’re raising boys: add to any activity an element of danger, stir in a little exploration, add a dash of destruction, and you’ve got yourself a winner. - John Eldredge OPTION 1. ACTIVITY: SNAZZY Many young men today don’t know how to be in formal environments. Much of modern life has become casual. When young men have to dress up, they pull at their ties, pick at their shirts, and feel uncomfortable. But if your son is going to thrive in environments that require reverence and respect, he needs to learn how to dress. Go to a store and pick out a tie together. It can be anywhere, but it has to be a real tie, not a zip-up. Let him pick the one he wants. Teach your son to tie a tie. Here is a nice British bloke showing you how: https://youtu.be/246VAGzQokw Then get dressed up in some of your nicest clothes. Go out to the nicest restaurant you can afford. Notice how different the atmosphere is from McDonald's. Get your son to list out all the differences he sees in a formal versus casual environment. Then talk about what it’s like to show respect to God and others. OPTION 2. WATCH Watch The Ten Commandments. A classic. Very dated, but solid if your son can concentrate. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KBzbyIPWVT4
FIELD NOTES PRAYER FOR MY SON
